task_completion.description: Open the custom waypoint creation tool or dialog.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box, menu, or input field specifically for creating a user-defined waypoint is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Enter the San Jose VOR (SJC) as the reference point.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The identifier 'SJC' is typed into the reference waypoint, facility, or origin field of the waypoint creation tool.

task_completion.description: Enter the 320-degree radial and 15 NM distance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The value '320' is entered into the radial/bearing field, and '15' is entered into the distance/DME field.

task_completion.description: Successfully generate or save the user-defined waypoint.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The waypoint is created and visible on the map, or its calculated coordinates (approximately 37.566 N, 122.147 W) are displayed in the software's waypoint properties.

task_completion.description: Add the newly created waypoint to the active flight plan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The active flight plan list or route sequence shows the newly created custom waypoint included as a leg in the route.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with a genuine flight planning graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a flight planning application (e.g., Little Navmap, SkyVector, ForeFlight, or a flight simulator GPS) rather than a terminal window or basic text editor.

integrity.description: The agent used the software's actual waypoint creation mechanism rather than just typing text.
integrity.visual_evidence: Inputs for SJC, 320, and 15 are entered into specific UI fields for radial/distance calculation, not just written as plain text in a generic text box.

integrity.description: The flight plan was modified within the application's native route planner.
integrity.visual_evidence: The waypoint appears in the application's official flight plan table or route list, complete with standard UI elements like distances, headings, or map lines connecting to it.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a waypoint using the San Jose VOR (SJC) as the reference, on the 320-degree radial at a distance of 15 NM. The correct resulting coordinates are approximately latitude 37.566°N, longitude 122.147°W (tolerance of 0.05 degrees / ~3 NM). Verify that the agent inputs SJC, 320, and 15 into the waypoint creation tool, and that the resulting waypoint is successfully added to the active flight plan.
